reasoning_chain_text: 1. For Harris, Hidalgo, Dallas, and Cameron counties, total day care home meal reimbursements in 2018-19 and 2020-21, compute the percentage decrease, and keep the two counties with the largest decreases.
2. For those two counties, compute average child-center enrollment across 2018-19 and 2019-20, then keep the county with the higher average.
4. Using that county seat, determine the coast associated with the selected county.
5. Return the gulf associated with that coast.
original_final_question: Among Harris, Hidalgo, Dallas, and Cameron counties in Texas, which two saw the largest percentage decrease in day care home meal reimbursements from 2018-19 to 2020-21? Of those two, which had the higher average child center enrollment across 2018-19 and 2019-20? For that county, what is the county seat, and which gulf does its coast border? Return the gulf name only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
original_reasoning_chain: HOP 1 (d=2, day care home reimbursements 2018-2019 vs 2020-2021 - nodes 1-2):
Percent decreases: Hidalgo -19.26%, Cameron -15.92%, Dallas -15.58%, Harris -13.69% -> two largest decreases {Hidalgo, Cameron}
HOP 2 (d=2, child center enrollment 2018-2019 vs 2019-2020 - nodes 3-4):
Average enrollment: Cameron 4,348,464.5 vs Hidalgo 1,569,466.5 -> Cameron County
Node 5: Cameron County seat is Brownsville
Node 6: Brownsville is on the Gulf Coast
Node 7: Gulf Coast is the coastline along the Gulf of Mexico

ideal_code.intent: Among {Harris, Hidalgo, Dallas, Cameron}, which counties had the largest percentage decrease in day care home meal reimbursements between the 2018-2019 and 2020-2021 program years? (Filter for this node: ProgramYear == 2018-2019; group by SiteCounty; sum TotalMealReimbursement.)
ideal_code.code:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.client import Config

source = 'datagov/summer-meal-programs-seamless-summer-option-sso-meal-count-information-program-period-2016/files/rows.txt'
bucket = 'lakeqa-yc4103-datalake'
obj = boto3.client('s3', config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj['Body'].read()), low_memory=False)
program_year = '2015-2016'
county = df['SiteCounty'].astype(str).str.strip().str.title()
lunch_total = pd.to_numeric(df['LunchTotal'], errors='coerce')
filtered = pd.DataFrame({'SiteCounty': county, 'LunchTotal': lunch_total})[df['ProgramYear'].astype(str).str.strip().eq(program_year) & county.ne('') & county.ne('Nan')].copy()
ranked = filtered.groupby('SiteCounty', as_index=False)['LunchTotal'].sum().sort_values(['LunchTotal', 'SiteCounty'], ascending=[False, True])
answer = ranked['SiteCounty'].head(5).tolist()

import io
import pandas as pd

from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.client import Config

ideal_code.intent: Among {Hidalgo, Cameron}, which county had the higher average child center enrollment across the 2018-2019 and 2019-2020 program years? (Filter for this node: ProgramYear == 2019-2020; group by SiteCounty; sum EnrollmentQty.)
ideal_code.code:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.client import Config

source = 'datagov/summer-meal-programs-seamless-summer-option-sso-meal-count-information-program-period-2019/files/rows.txt'
bucket = 'lakeqa-yc4103-datalake'
obj = boto3.client('s3', config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj['Body'].read()), low_memory=False)
program_year = '2018-2019'
county = df['SiteCounty'].astype(str).str.strip().str.title()
lunch_total = pd.to_numeric(df['LunchTotal'], errors='coerce')
filtered = pd.DataFrame({'SiteCounty': county, 'LunchTotal': lunch_total})[df['ProgramYear'].astype(str).str.strip().eq(program_year) & county.ne('') & county.ne('Nan')].copy()
ranked = filtered.groupby('SiteCounty', as_index=False)['LunchTotal'].sum().sort_values(['LunchTotal', 'SiteCounty'], ascending=[False, True])
answer = ranked['SiteCounty'].head(5).tolist()

ideal_query.intent: Among {Harris, Hidalgo, Dallas, Cameron}, which counties had the largest percentage decrease in day care home meal reimbursements between the 2018-2019 and 2020-2021 program years? (Filter for this node: ProgramYear == 2018-2019; group by SiteCounty; sum TotalMealReimbursement.)
ideal_query.sql:
WITH filtered AS (
  SELECT CASE "SiteCounty"
           WHEN 'HARRIS' THEN 'Harris'
           WHEN 'DALLAS' THEN 'Dallas'
           WHEN 'HIDALGO' THEN 'Hidalgo'
           WHEN 'CAMERON' THEN 'Cameron'
         END AS county,
         TRY_CAST("TotalMealReimbursement" AS DOUBLE) AS reimbursement
  FROM t
  WHERE "ProgramYear" = '2018-2019'
    AND "SiteCounty" IN ('HARRIS', 'DALLAS', 'HIDALGO', 'CAMERON')
), totals AS (
  SELECT county, ROUND(SUM(reimbursement), 2) AS total
  FROM filtered
  GROUP BY county
)
SELECT json_group_object(county, total) AS answer FROM totals

ideal_query.intent: Among {Harris, Hidalgo, Dallas, Cameron}, which counties had the largest percentage decrease in day care home meal reimbursements between the 2018-2019 and 2020-2021 program years? (Filter for this node: ProgramYear == 2020-2021; group by SiteCounty; sum TotalMealReimbursement.)
ideal_query.sql:
WITH filtered AS (
  SELECT CASE "SiteCounty"
           WHEN 'HARRIS' THEN 'Harris'
           WHEN 'DALLAS' THEN 'Dallas'
           WHEN 'HIDALGO' THEN 'Hidalgo'
           WHEN 'CAMERON' THEN 'Cameron'
         END AS county,
         TRY_CAST("TotalMealReimbursement" AS DOUBLE) AS reimbursement
  FROM t
  WHERE "ProgramYear" = '2020-2021'
    AND "SiteCounty" IN ('HARRIS', 'DALLAS', 'HIDALGO', 'CAMERON')
), totals AS (
  SELECT county, ROUND(SUM(reimbursement), 2) AS total
  FROM filtered
  GROUP BY county
)
SELECT json_group_object(county, total) AS answer FROM totals

ideal_query.intent: Among {Hidalgo, Cameron}, which county had the higher average child center enrollment across the 2018-2019 and 2019-2020 program years? (Filter for this node: ProgramYear == 2018-2019; group by SiteCounty; sum EnrollmentQty.)
ideal_query.sql:
WITH filtered AS (
  SELECT CASE "SiteCounty"
           WHEN 'CAMERON' THEN 'Cameron'
           WHEN 'HIDALGO' THEN 'Hidalgo'
         END AS county,
         TRY_CAST("EnrollmentQty" AS BIGINT) AS enrollment
  FROM t
  WHERE "ProgramYear" = '2018-2019'
    AND "SiteCounty" IN ('CAMERON', 'HIDALGO')
), totals AS (
  SELECT county, CAST(SUM(enrollment) AS BIGINT) AS total
  FROM filtered
  GROUP BY county
)
SELECT json_group_object(county, total) AS answer FROM totals

ideal_query.intent: Among {Hidalgo, Cameron}, which county had the higher average child center enrollment across the 2018-2019 and 2019-2020 program years? (Filter for this node: ProgramYear == 2019-2020; group by SiteCounty; sum EnrollmentQty.)
ideal_query.sql:
WITH filtered AS (
  SELECT CASE "SiteCounty"
           WHEN 'CAMERON' THEN 'Cameron'
           WHEN 'HIDALGO' THEN 'Hidalgo'
         END AS county,
         TRY_CAST("EnrollmentQty" AS BIGINT) AS enrollment

    AND "SiteCounty" IN ('CAMERON', 'HIDALGO')
), totals AS (
  SELECT county, CAST(SUM(enrollment) AS BIGINT) AS total
  FROM filtered

)
SELECT json_group_object(county, total) AS answer FROM totals
